summary: 2026-06-07 11:33:18 PDT - Reviewed `git diff main...HEAD`, `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, and `reviews/camera-overlay-diagnostics.md`. The instrumentation is mostly placed on the right lifecycle points, but I found privacy and observe-only risks that should be fixed before treating this as clean.

findings.title: init-error log sends non-allowlisted error text
findings.claim: The story's privacy contract limits new lifecycle logs to ids, running-context strings, event names, and payload shape/status. This new `init-error` lifecycle log sends `err?.message ?? String(err)` to `/api/log`, which is arbitrary SDK/init error text and is outside that allowlist.
findings.suggestion: Keep the local `console.warn` if needed, but make the lifecycle log allowlisted, e.g. `{ mode: result.mode, status: 'failed' }`, or only include a deliberately sanitized/allowlisted SDK error code.

findings.title: overlay-message logging can throw before state update
findings.claim: The payload shape is computed before entering `logLifecycle`, so `Object.keys(payload)` or `payload?.status` can throw before the helper's try/catch runs. If that happens, the `onMessage` handler never reaches `setState(payload)`, so diagnostics can change receive-side behavior instead of observing it.
findings.suggestion: Move payload-shape extraction behind a small safe helper or local try/catch, default to a minimal `{ received: true }`/`keys: null` log on failure, and ensure `setState(payload)` still runs.
